title: Dog Whining When Ear Is Touched: Causes and Next Steps
meta_desc: Find out why your dog whines when their ear is touched, learn about urgency levels, and see how a triage photo can help your veterinarian.
content_html:
What is causing your dog's ear pain?

When a dog whines or reacts painfully to having the base of their ear touched, it is usually a sign of inflammation or infection within the ear canal, known as otitis externa. This sensitivity can be caused by bacterial or yeast infections, ear mites, or even a foreign object like a grass seed lodged deep inside. Because the ear canal in dogs is L-shaped, debris and moisture can easily become trapped, leading to significant pressure and discomfort that makes even a light touch feel agonizing for your pet.

Is this an emergency?

The urgency level for ear pain is generally classified as Medium. While it is rarely a life-threatening emergency, ear pain is intense and distressing for dogs. You should aim to schedule a veterinary appointment within 24 to 48 hours. However, if your dog is also showing signs of a head tilt, loss of balance, or rapid eye movements, this could indicate an inner ear infection or neurological issue that requires more immediate care.

Why a photo helps with triage

Taking a clear photo of the inside of the ear flap and the entrance to the canal can be incredibly helpful for a triage professional. A photo allows a veterinarian to look for specific visual cues like 'cobblestone' texture, dark discharge resembling coffee grounds (common with mites), or bright red swelling. These visual markers help determine how quickly your dog needs to be seen and whether there is an immediate risk of a ruptured eardrum.

faqs.question: Can I put ear drops in my dog's ear before the vet visit?
faqs.answer: No, you should avoid putting any liquids or medications into the ear until a veterinarian has confirmed the eardrum is intact. If the eardrum is ruptured, certain cleaners and medications can cause permanent deafness or vestibular damage.

faqs.question: What are common signs of a dog ear infection besides whining?
faqs.answer: In addition to vocalizing, you may notice frequent head shaking, scratching at the ear, a foul odor, or visible discharge that is yellow, brown, or bloody.

faqs.question: How do vets diagnose the cause of ear pain?
faqs.answer: A veterinarian will use an otoscope to look down into the canal and may take a swab (cytology) to look under a microscope for bacteria, yeast, or parasites.
